introSection: Woodstock Express is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA. It was built by Chance Rides and opened in 1999 as Big Dipper.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
When the ride first opened in 1999, it was originally known as Big Dipper.

In August 2019, it was announced that a Camp Snoopy area would be added to Michigan's Adventure. As part of the new area, Big Dipper would be renamed Woodstock Express. It was set to open in 2020, but this was delayed due to the coronavirus pandemic. However, Camp Snoopy would open on May 29, 2021.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yellow track and black supports, used to be blue track and white supports.

fullText:
Woodstock Express is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Michigan's Adventure in Muskegon, Michigan, USA. It was built by Chance Rides and opened in 1999 as Big Dipper.

When the ride first opened in 1999, it was originally known as Big Dipper.

In August 2019, it was announced that a Camp Snoopy area would be added to Michigan's Adventure. As part of the new area, Big Dipper would be renamed Woodstock Express. It was set to open in 2020, but this was delayed due to the coronavirus pandemic. However, Camp Snoopy would open on May 29, 2021.



Yellow track and black supports, used to be blue track and white supports.
